title: TSMC’s AI Boom Is Just Getting Started: Why Investors Should Buy the Pullback in TSM Stock Now
excerpt: TSMC earnings, AI demand, and analyst targets point higher. What does this mean for investors?
content_html:
With artificial intelligence fueling upside, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) is still one of the hottest tech giants to buy and hold long-term. Furthermore, the company just posted strong earnings. EPS of $4.31 beat by $0.37. Revenue of $40.2 billion rocketed 33.7% higher year-over-year (YOY), beating estimates by $300 million. And analysts are still incredibly bullish. For example, Wedbush reiterated an "Outperform" rating on the stock, with a price target of NT$3,000 from NT$2,900, thanks to strong, growing demand for artificial intelligence chips.

And while the stock did pull back on the company's plans to increase its 2026 capital expenditures to between $60 billion and $64 billion, up from $52 billion to $56 billion, I'd use recent weakness as an opportunity to buy for the long haul. 

More News from Barchart

  • This Dividend King Could Be One of the Safest Stocks to Own Right Now

  • AI Startup Moonshot Just Launched Its Record-Setting Kimi K3 Model. Buy Alibaba Stock to Benefit.

  • As the AI Selloff Worsens, Time-Tested Procter & Gamble Stock Could Be the Biggest Winner

  • Tired of missing midday reversals? The FREE Barchart Brief newsletter keeps you in the know. Sign up now!

TSM's Full-Year Growth Outlook is Strong

The company now expects revenue to grow by more than 40% in 2026, which is higher than the 30% it had previously forecast. Better, TSMC Chairman and CEO C.C. Wei added that "The AI megatrend continues to drive the need for more and more computation, which supports the robust demand for leading-edge silicon. Our customers and customers-at-customers, who are mainly the cloud service providers, continue to provide us with their very strong signals and positive outlook. Thus, our conviction in the multi-year AI megatrend remains very high."

In addition, the company announced plans to invest another $100 billion in its foundries in Arizona. CEO C.C. Wei says the investment will help fund advanced packaging facilities and the production of 2-nanometer chips. He added that the investment will help grow the U.S. semiconductor industry and strengthen supply chains. Plus, TSMC will continue investing in Taiwan by building 13 advanced manufacturing and packaging facilities over the next several years. He added that continued investments in both the U.S. and Taiwan will help the company maintain an important role in the global semiconductor industry while supporting innovation from its customers.

TSM Is a Key Player in the AI Revolution

The biggest reason investors are excited about TSMC is its connection to the AI boom. TSMC is the world's leading chip manufacturer. It makes advanced semiconductors for some of the biggest technology companies in the world, including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia Corporation (NVDA), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), to name a few. Many companies design their own chips but rely upon TSMC to actually produce them.

sentences.zh: 台积电是全球领先的芯片制造商。

content_html_zh:
随着人工智能的推动，台积电（TSM）仍然是最热门的长期买入和持有科技巨头之一。 此外，该公司刚刚公布了强劲的收益。 每股收益 4.31 美元，上涨 0.37 美元。 收入达 402 亿美元，同比增长 33.7%，超出预期 3 亿美元。 分析师仍然非常乐观。 例如，Wedbush 重申对该股的“跑赢大盘”评级，目标股价从新台币 2,900 元上调至新台币 3,000 元，原因是人工智能芯片需求强劲且不断增长。

尽管该股确实取消了该公司将 2026 年资本支出从 520 亿美元增至 560 亿美元至 600 亿至 640 亿美元的计划，但我仍会利用近期的疲软作为长期买入的机会。

来自条形图的更多新闻

  • 这种股息之王可能是目前最安全的股票之一
  • AI 初创公司 Moonshot 刚刚推出了创纪录的 Kimi K3 模型。 购买阿里巴巴股票即可受益。
  • 随着人工智能抛售加剧，经过时间考验的宝洁股票可能成为最大赢家
  • 厌倦了错过午间反转吗？ 免费的条形图简讯让您了解最新情况。 立即注册！

TSM 全年增长前景强劲

该公司目前预计2026年收入将增长40%以上，高于此前预测的30%。 更好的是，台积电董事长兼首席执行官 C.C. 魏补充道：“人工智能大趋势继续推动对越来越多计算的需求，这支持了对尖端芯片的强劲需求。 我们的客户和客户客户（主要是云服务提供商）继续向我们提供非常强烈的信号和积极的前景。 因此，我们对多年人工智能大趋势的信心仍然非常高。”

此外，该公司还宣布计划再投资1000亿美元对其位于亚利桑那州的代工厂进行投资。 首席执行官 C.C. Wei 表示，这笔投资将有助于资助先进封装设施和 2 纳米芯片的生产。 他补充说，这项投资将有助于发展美国半导体产业并加强供应链。 此外，台积电将在未来几年继续在台湾投资，建设 13 座先进制造和封装工厂。 他补充说，在美国和台湾的持续投资将有助于该公司在全球半导体行业保持重要地位，同时支持客户的创新。

TSM 是人工智能革命的关键参与者

投资者对台积电感到兴奋的最大原因是它与人工智能热潮的联系。 台积电是全球领先的芯片制造商。 它为世界上一些最大的科技公司生产先进的半导体，包括苹果公司 (AAPL)、英伟达公司 (NVDA) 和 Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) 等。 许多公司自己设计芯片，但实际生产却依赖台积电。
